Headline: Stenoptilia himachala
Description: Stenoptilia himachala is a moth of the family Pterophoridae. It is found in Himachal Pradesh, India.[1]

Headline: Alaska Commercial Company
Description: Alaska Commercial Company (ACC) is a grocery and retail company which operates stores in rural Alaska, beginning in the early period of Alaska's ownership by the United States into the present.[1] From 1901 to 1992, it was known as the Northern Commercial Company (NCC), and in 1992 it resumed business as the Alaska Commercial Company under the ownership of the North West Company.

Headline: Ernest Eugene Kramer
Description: Ernest Eugene Kramer (10 May 1889 – 16 February 1958) was a non-denominational itinerant missionary who worked in Central Australia, mostly Alice Springs, from 1913 until 1934 who is known for his camel train caravan mission.[1][2] Kramer was responsible for building the first church in Alice Springs, the Ebenezer Tabernacle (which was commonly known as Kramer's Church).[3]

Headline: Joshua N. Goldberg
Description: Joshua N. Goldberg (May 30, 1925 – October 5, 2020) was an American physicist and educator who was particularly noted for his research on general relativity.[2][3][4]
